summary: Backend API smoke tests all pass (8/8). Landing page renders correctly with hero/CTAs/eyebrow. Auth flow is BLOCKED: (1) Supabase project has email confirmation enabled and hit `over_email_send_rate_limit` so new signups cannot complete; (2) pre-existing test accounts in /app/memory/test_credentials.md do NOT exist on the project (invalid_credentials); (3) a frontend error-handling bug displays 'Failed to execute json on Response: body stream already read' instead of the real Supabase error, masking the actual issue from users. Because auth is blocked, dashboard, QuizBuilder, QuizPlayer, Results, sign-out flows could NOT be exercised end-to-end.

ui_bugs.issue: On any auth failure (sign-in or sign-up), the inline error shows 'Failed to execute json on Response: body stream already read' instead of the real Supabase error message (e.g. 'Invalid login credentials' or 'email rate limit exceeded'). The response body is being consumed twice somewhere in the supabase client/fetch chain. Users get no actionable info.

integration_issues.issue: Supabase returned 429 over_email_send_rate_limit. This indicates email confirmation is ENABLED on project iavfegyovaictppxrrvz, contrary to /app/memory/test_credentials.md guidance. Must be disabled in Supabase dashboard, or a confirmed seed account must be provisioned, otherwise signup-then-dashboard happy path is not testable.
test-creator-rocketsense@example.com / RocketTest123! returns 400 invalid_credentials — the account does not exist on the Supabase project. Either signup never ran or it was never confirmed.

action_items: Disable email confirmation in Supabase project (Auth -> Providers -> Email) OR pre-create a confirmed test account using the service role key on startup so testers can sign in.

Fix the 'body stream already read' error surfacing in AuthPage. Likely caused by reading the fetch Response body more than once (or a global fetch interceptor). Inspect any custom fetch wrappers passed to supabase.createClient, axios-like wrappers, or service worker patches. Ensure err.message from supabase-js is shown directly (supabase-js already returns parsed AuthApiError objects — no manual response.json() should be needed).
Wait for the Supabase email rate-limit cool-down (it lasts ~1 hour) OR use a different test mailbox domain before re-testing signup.
Update /app/memory/test_credentials.md once accounts/settings are correct so the next testing agent can sign in deterministically.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend server.py: uses os.environ[KEY] for SUPABASE_URL/KEY (good — fails fast). load_dotenv before reads — OK.
Backend grade_answer for type=short returns True for any non-empty text — this is intentional per comment ('reflective'), but means short questions auto-score correct. Worth a product confirmation.
Backend update_quiz returns merged dict that may still include legacy _id from `existing` — but existing was fetched with projection {_id:0}, so safe.
Frontend auth error UX: real supabase error is being swallowed. Highest priority UX bug.
No retry/backoff or user-friendly handling for Supabase 429 rate limits.
test_credentials: test-creator-rocketsense@example.com / RocketTest123! -> DOES NOT EXIST on Supabase (invalid_credentials). New signups currently blocked by Supabase rate limit.
seed_data_creation: None — could not create accounts due to Supabase rate limit + email confirm.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_backend_api.py — keep using as regression. The dashboard/builder/player/results/signout flows are still untested. Once main agent (a) disables Supabase email confirm or seeds a confirmed account and (b) fixes the AuthPage error rendering (body stream already read), re-run the full Playwright happy path: signup -> dashboard -> create quiz (all 5 question types selectable in question-type-select-0) -> play_quiz_<id> -> question-overlay -> overlay-submit-button -> quiz-submit-button -> results-score-card 100% -> nav-signout-button.
rca of the issue: Root cause 1: Supabase project has 'Confirm email' enabled and the auth provider's hourly email send rate-limit is exhausted (429 over_email_send_rate_limit at /auth/v1/signup). Reproduction: POST any new email to /auth/v1/signup -> 429. Mitigation: disable email confirm in Supabase Auth settings, or use the service role key to admin-create a confirmed user; wait out the rate limit (~1h). Root cause 2 (frontend): The error rendered in AuthPage is 'body stream already read'. supabase-js v2 returns parsed AuthApiError objects so calling err.message should already give the textual reason — the fact that this DOM string is appearing means somewhere a Response body is being .json()'d twice (likely a custom global fetch wrapper). Mitigation: audit any global fetch/Request monkeypatches, remove duplicate body reads, ensure supabase.createClient is given the unmodified global fetch.
